The fact that the loan duration is longer as longer maturity time increases default probability, because longer maturity time increases default probability, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [1]
The fact that there is no checking account as lacking a checking account is associated with lower estimated risk, because lacking a checking account is associated with lower estimated risk, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [2]
The fact that the savings balance is low as savings account information helps classify borrowers into risk groups, because savings account information helps classify borrowers into risk groups, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.
